title: How to Talk about Videogames
creator: Ian Bogost
description: A collection of short critical essays that examines particular games, mechanics, objects, and habits with close attention. The subjects range from major releases to mundane and unfashionable games.
useful: It models criticism that begins with what a game actually does instead of using games only as examples of a preselected theory.
audience: Beginning critics, developers, and general readers who want to write more precisely about games.
learning: Build an argument from close attention to a game
Write about mechanics alongside culture and material objects
Choose a critical scale appropriate to the subject
